notes: Rev 2 — single Railway service. Layer 2 behavioral validation run against the COMPILED server (node dist/src/index.js) in NODE_ENV=production against the local Docker Postgres (:8765, DATABASE_SSL=false), PORT=4010, SERVE_STATIC=true, RUN_SCOUT_WORKER=true. The live Railway URL items (T7-T9) remain pending the browser-driven provisioning and are listed as such; they do not gate this local-prod artifact because no Railway project exists yet (the orchestrator provisions).

items.title: Compiled prod server boots: binds 0.0.0.0:$PORT, serves SPA, starts in-process worker
items.precondition: npm run build done (frontend/dist + dist/ present); local Docker Postgres up on :8765 with the schema migrated
steps: 1. NODE_ENV=production DATABASE_URL=<local docker> DATABASE_SSL=false DATABASE_POOL_MODE=persistent PORT=4010 SERVE_STATIC=true RUN_SCOUT_WORKER=true node dist/src/index.js
items.expected: Logs: 'Serving SPA static build', 'Starting in-process scout worker', and 'PM Tool API listening on 0.0.0.0:4010' with serveStatic:true, runScoutWorker:true
items.evidence: Log showed spaDir=/.../frontend/dist; 'Scout worker started (local poll loop)'; 'PM Tool API listening on 0.0.0.0:4010' env=production serveStatic=true runScoutWorker=true

items.title: GET /health (root alias) returns the success envelope
items.expected: HTTP 200, application/json, { success:true, data:{ status:'ok', database:'up', timestamp }, error:null }
items.notes: Root /health alias is what Railway's healthcheckPath pings.

items.title: GET /api/health (SPA same-origin API path) returns the success envelope
items.expected: HTTP 200, application/json, success envelope with database:'up'
items.evidence: HTTP 200 application/json; success envelope database:'up' — confirms the API is reachable under /api same-origin

items.title: GET / and a deep route return the SPA index.html (client routing)
items.expected: / and /dashboard/123 → HTTP 200 text/html (the SPA shell); the hashed asset → 200 application/javascript
items.evidence: GET / → 200 HTML (<!doctype html> ... <title>PM Ticket Tool</title>); GET /dashboard/123 → 200 HTML; GET /assets/index-BTrRNmrU.js → 200 application/javascript
items.notes: Client-routing catch-all serves index.html for non-API GETs.

items.title: Unknown API path returns the 404 JSON envelope, NOT the SPA HTML
items.expected: HTTP 404, application/json, { success:false, error:{ code:'NOT_FOUND', ... } } — never index.html
items.notes: Proves the catch-all correctly excludes /api so API 404s stay JSON (§8.1).

items.title: In-process scout worker claims and drains an enqueued job (no cron / no HTTP trigger)
items.precondition: T1 server running with RUN_SCOUT_WORKER=true; a scout_jobs 'pending' row inserted directly in the DB (session_id valid, provider=github, repo_ref=expressjs/cors)
steps: 1. INSERT a session + a pending scout_jobs row directly via psql
2. Poll scout_jobs.status for that job id for ~20s
3. Check scout_cache for a row for the session
items.expected: Within ~15s the job moves pending→running→done (attempts=1, no error) and a scout_cache row with findings is written — driven by the in-process worker, not the internal HTTP endpoint
items.evidence: Job id 92 (session 2565): claimed within the first poll, reached status 'done' at t=17s, attempts=1, last_error null; scout_cache row id 150 written with real findings for expressjs/cors. Worker logs: 'Scout job claimed; running bounded scan' then 'Scout job completed; findings cached' (§21.4). Graceful SIGTERM then logged 'Scout worker stopped'.
items.notes: Used a real bounded scan of the public expressjs/cors repo. ~17s is dominated by the model/scan call; the claim itself was immediate (race-safe FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, §21.1).

items.title: LIVE Railway: app reachable on the deployment URL backed by Railway Postgres
items.precondition: Railway project provisioned, GitHub connected, env vars set, deployed (browser-driven; orchestrator provisions)
items.expected: The SPA loads; /health returns the success envelope backed by Railway Postgres
items.notes: Pending live provisioning — no Railway account is touched by this code task.

items.title: LIVE Railway: push to main auto-deploys; migrations applied to Railway Postgres
steps: 1. Push a trivial commit to main
3. Confirm start:prod ran knex migrate:latest before the server bound
items.expected: A push to main triggers a Railway build/deploy; the pre-start migrate applies all migrations to Railway Postgres before traffic

items.title: LIVE Railway: end-to-end smoke (session → interview → ticket) + scout drains in-process
items.precondition: T7, T8; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set in Railway
steps: 1. Create a session in the live SPA
4. Trigger a scout and poll until done
items.expected: Session persists in Railway Postgres; the interview returns questions; a ticket is generated and stored; the scout job is drained by the in-process worker (no cron)
